sections.paragraph:
συμβάλλω impf. συνέβαλλον; 2 aor. συνέβαλον; pf. συμβέβληκα. Mid.: 2 aor. συνεβαλόμην; pf. 3 sg. συμβέβληται Wsd 5:8. Pass.: aor. 3 sg. συνεβλήθη Sir 22:1f (Hom. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; TestGad 4:4)

① to engage in mutual pondering of a matter, converse, confer (w. λόγους added Eur., Iphig. Aul. 830; without λ. Plut., Mor. 222c) τινί with someone (Epict. 4, 12, 7; Iambl., Vi. Pyth. 2, 12; PFay 129, 2) Ac 17:18. πρὸς ἀλλήλους 4:15.

② to give careful thought to, consider, ponder (Pla., Crat. 384a μαντείαν; Philo, In Flacc. 139; Jos., Ant. 2, 72 συμβαλὼν τῷ λογισμῷ τὸ ὄναρ) τὰ ῥήματα συμβάλλουσα ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτῆς Lk 2:19 (cp. our colloquial ‘get it all together’).

③ to draw a conclusion by comparing, compare (Hdt. et al.; pap; Sir 22:1f; Jos., Ant. 1, 105) τινά τινι someone with someth. ἑαυτὸν ξύλῳ 1 Cl 23:4=2 Cl 11:3 (quot. of unknown orig.).

④ to come together at a point, meet, fall in with (Hom. et al.; pap) τινί someone (on a journey; cp. Jos., Ant. 1, 219; 2, 184; Just., D. 68, 1; 80, 4) Ac 20:14. Cp. MPol 8:1.

⑤ to come into conflict w. someone

ⓐ engage, fight someone τινί (Polyb. 1, 9, 7; 3, 111, 1 al.; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 16, 6; 1 Macc 4:34; 2 Macc 8:23; 14:17) εἰς πόλεμον meet someone in battle, wage war on someone Lk 14:31 (cp. εἰς μάχην Polyb. 3, 56, 6; Jos., Bell. 1, 191, Ant. 12, 342; πρὸς μάχην Polyb. 10, 37, 4).

ⓑ quarrel, dispute τινί with someone (PSI 93, 4 συνέβαλον τοῖς ἐπιτρόποις) συμβάλλειν αὐτῷ περὶ πλειόνων quarrel with him about many things Lk 11:53 v.l.

⑥ to be of assistance, help, be of assistance, mid. (Philo, Migr. Abr. 219; Just., D. 46, 7) τινί (to) someone (Pla.; Demosth. 21, 133; Antiphon 5, 79 p. 138, 37 πολλὰ συμβ. τοῖς βουλομένοις; Polyb. 2, 13, 1; Epict. 3, 22, 78 πλείονά τινι ς.; PLond VI, 1915, 13; Wsd 5:8; Jos., Ant. 12, 312; Ar. [Milne 74, 22]; Tat. 24, 1) Apollos συνεβάλετο πολὺ τοῖς πεπιστευκόσιν Ac 18:27. συνεβάλλοντο … πάντες οἱ ἀδελφοὶ ὡς μηδὲν λυπηθῆναι Παῦλον all the fellowship were helping (as much as possible) … that Paul should not be grieved AcPl Ha 7, 17.—M-M s.v. συνβάλλω. EDNT.

impf. impf. = imperfect

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. sg. = singular

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Iambl Iambl , phil., III–IV a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

quot. quot. = quotation

orig. orig. = original(ly)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 956
